content: We can frame zero-shot node generalization in graph contrastive pre-training as an intrinsic exploration problem for the graph encoder, and use a modified ICM curiosity mechanism to reward the encoder for discovering out-of-distribution node embeddings that have high structural and semantic surprise during pre-training. This creates an intrinsic reward signal that complements existing cross-modal contrastive losses to improve zero-shot generalization to unseen node categories that do not appear in pre-training graph data.
source_topics: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
trigger_topic: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
